wiki.extract: The Columbia Sportswear Company is an American company that manufactures and distributes outerwear, sportswear, and footwear, as well as headgear, camping equipment, ski apparel, and outerwear accessories.

topHazards.label: The batteries have a cell defect which can cause overheating, posing a fire hazard.
The heated inner wrist cuff can overheat, posing a burn hazard.

sampleRecalls.title: Columbia Sportswear Recalls Seven Models of Heated Jackets Due To Burn Hazard
sampleRecalls.description: This recall involves seven models of men's and women's 2012 Columbia Omni-Heat™ electric heated jackets. They are polyester and have the Columbia name printed on the front upper left side. The jackets have two battery packs located in inner pouches. Jackets with the following names and style numbers are being recalled…
hazards: The heated inner wrist cuff can overheat, posing a burn hazard.
injuries: No incidents of jacket wrist cuffs overheating were reported in the U.S. The firm received reports of one incident in Europe and one in Canada. No injuries were reported.
units: About 9,600 in the U.S. and 640 in Canada

sampleRecalls.title: Columbia Sportswear Reannounces Its Recall of Batteries Sold With Jackets Due To Fire Hazard
sampleRecalls.description: This recall involves battery packs that power heating systems in jackets. The black battery packs are 3.25 inches long by 2.3 inches wide by 0.7 inches deep and marked with "Columbia" on the top and "OMNI-HEAT™" on the bottom of the pack. Part number 054978-001 is printed on the side of the battery label. Two battery …
hazards: The batteries have a cell defect which can cause overheating, posing a fire hazard.
injuries: The firm received one report of an overheating battery in Europe. No incidents or injuries were reported in the U.S.
units: About 66 batteries (33 jackets with two battery packs each)

sampleRecalls.title: Columbia Sportswear Recalls Batteries Sold With Jackets Due To Fire Hazard
sampleRecalls.description: This recall involves battery packs that power heating systems in jackets. Two battery packs were included with the following Omni-Heat™ Electric jacket styles: Omni-Heat™ Electric Wader Widgeon, Omni-Heat™ Electric Big Game and Omni-Heat™ Gale Warning Interchange. The black battery packs are 3.25 inches long by 2.3 in…
hazards: The batteries have a cell defect which can cause overheating, posing a fire hazard.
injuries: The firm has received one report of a battery overheating that was discovered at its distribution facility in France. No incidents or injuries have been reported in the United States.
units: About 220 (two battery packs sold with each jacket totaling 440 batteries)
